Subject: Statistisk sentralbyr├е (SSB) inviterer deg til Boligpanelet i desember
Body: Til Bree 0581 OSLO Hei, Vi setter stor pris p├е at du deltok i Boligpanelet forrige m├еned. Det er n├е tid for ├е sende inn denne m├еnedens opplysninger. Det er viktig at du svarer selv om det ikke har v├жrt noen endringer i husleien. Unders├╕kelsen er sv├жrt kort, og det tar bare et par minutter ├е svare. Benytt lenken nedenfor for ├е svare p├е unders├╕kelsen for november: https://svar.ssb.no/bolig/ Lenken logger deg automatisk inn. Om du blir bedt om ├е logge inn, skriv inn: ID: Dersom du ikke har anledning til ├е svare p├е web innen mandag neste uke, vil en av v├еre intervjuere ringe deg med tilbud om ├е gjennomf├╕re intervjuet p├е telefon. Svarene dine er viktige Din bolig en av 2 500 boliger som er trukket ut til ├е svare. Boligen du bor i skal v├жre med i unders├╕kelsen til og med oktober 2025, og vi vil kontakte deg en gang i m├еneden for ├е sp├╕rre om hva du betaler i husleie. Det er frivillig ├е delta i unders├╕kelsen, men svarene du gir er sv├жrt viktig for ├е kunne bergene konsumprisindeksen. Konsumprisindeksen viser prisutviklingen i Norge og publiseres hver m├еned. For ├е kunne m├еle m├еnedlige endringer m├е vi ha svar hver m├еned. Derfor er det viktig at du deltar hver gang, selv om ikke husleien har endret seg. Det er ogs├е viktig ├е svare om du har flyttet fra boligen, slik at vi kan unders├╕ke om den st├еr tom eller har f├еtt en ny beboer. Du kan lese mer om unders├╕kelsen her. Opplysningene dine er sikre hos oss Svarene dine bruker vi kun til ├е lage statistikk og enkeltsvar vil aldri bli offentliggjort. Du kan n├еr som helst trekke deg og be om at opplysningen om deg blir slettet. Dette gj├╕r du ved ├е ringe eller sende oss en e-post. Innen 1. desember 2026 vil alle opplysningene om deg bli anonymisert. Kontakt oss p├е e-post: svar@ssb.no telefon: 62 88 56 08 Svartjenesten er ├еpen kl. 09тАУ14 mandag til fredag. Tusen takk for at du deltar! Med vennlig hilsen Geir Axelsen administrerende direkt├╕r Dear Sir or Madam, Statistics Norway appreciates your participation in last monthтАЩs Housing Panel. It is now time for this monthтАЩs follow-up questionnaire. It is important that you respond even though there have been no changes in the rent. The survey is very short, and it only takes a couple of minutes to answer. Use the link below to answer the survey: https://svar.ssb.no/bolig/ The link will log you in automatically. If you are asked to log on, please use the following information: Id: If you donтАЩt have the opportunity answer the survey on web within Monday next week, an interviewer will call you to offer a telephone interview. Your answers are valuable Your dwelling is one of 2 500 dwellings sampled from the survey. The dwelling you live in will be included in the survey until October 2025, and we will contact you once a month to ask about what you pay for rent. It is voluntarily to participate in the survey, but the answers you provide are important in calculating the Consumer Price Index. The Consumer Price Index shows price trends in Norway and is published every month. To measure monthly changes, we need answers every month. Therefore, it is important that you participate every time, even if the rent has not changed. It is also important to reply even if you have moved from the address, so we can find the new tenant. You can read more about the survey here. Your information is secure The answers you provide will only be used to compile statistics, and individual responses will never be published. You may withdraw from the survey at any time and have your answers are deleted. You can do this by calling or sending us an e-mail. By 1 December 2026, all information about you will be anonymised. Contact us at e-mail: svar@ssb.no telephone: 62 88 56 08 Our support service is open 9amтАУ2pm Monday to Friday. Thank you for participating! Best regards, Geir Axelsen Director general

Subject: The Graduation I Wish I Had
Body: Forbes NewslettersPlus: The WorldтАЩs Highest Paid Athletes 2023ADVERTISEMENTBECOME A MEMBER тАв Newsletters тАв MyForbesGood morning, Jupiter will be eclipsed by the Moon for the last time this week until 2026. Back on Earth, we're covering Microsoft's purchase of gaming giant Activision Blizzard, Florida's ban on funding DEI programs in public colleges and Bill Gates' five gems of advice he wished "I was told at the graduation I never had."Have a great day, Caroline Howard Executive Editor Follow me on Twitter and Forbes.comBREAKING NEWSGETTY IMAGESWells Fargo has agreed to pay $1 billion to settle a class action suit over the way it handled the cleanup of its phony account scandal, according to court documents. ItтАЩs the latest bill in a series of large settlements as the bank continues to manage the fallout from the scheme nearly a decade after its discovery.A report by Special Counsel John Durham found the FBI should not have launched a full investigation into the connections between former President Donald TrumpтАЩs campaign and Russia during the 2016 election. The report said the FBI used тАЬraw, unanalyzed and uncorroborated intelligenceтАЭ to justify the investigation.TECH & INNOVATIONEuropean Union regulators approved Microsoft's whopping $69 billion bid for video game developer Activision Blizzard, providing a ray of hope for what would be the biggest tech deal in decades. It comes less than a month after regulators in the United Kingdom blocked the massive acquisition over concerns it would stifle competition.Loop Capital downgraded its stock rating for Google parent Alphabet from a buy to hold in a buzzy client note. The skepticism stems from concerns about how generative AI chatbots like Microsoft-backed ChatGPT question GoogleтАЩs long-standing status as the primary gateway to the web.MONEY + POLITICSA woman suing Rudy Giuliani says that the former New York City mayor spoke about selling presidential pardons for $2 million and splitting the money with Donald Trump. The accuser made the claim as part of a lawsuit that says Giuliani hired her, forced her into sexual activities, failed to pay her salary and then fired her.Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis signed a bill to stop public colleges from funding diversity, equity and inclusion programs and limit how race can be discussed in university classrooms, marking the first legislation of its kind to be signed into law nationwideтАФall part of a broader push by DeSantis to restrict race-related education.SCIENCE + HEALTHCAREAFP VIA GETTY IMAGESWalgreens remains committed to clinical trials despite rival CVS HealthтАЩs decision to wind down its trials by the end of next year. Both pharmacy chains launched clinical trials in the past two years when the FDA was looking to improve drug research and patient health outcomes by enrolling more diverse participants.It's a great week for stargazing in May. The new Moon means dark night skies. Arguably the most fascinating sight will be a waning crescent moon beside giant JupiterтАФand, if you're in the right place, you'll see Jupiter disappear behind the Moon.SPORTS + ENTERTAINMENTTimbaland VARIETY VIA GETTY IMAGESFresh off his controversial "collaboration" with the late rapper Biggie Smalls, previewed on Instagram this month, the Grammy-winning music producer Timbaland told Forbes he has a plan to commercialize artificial intelligence software to revolutionize how songs are made. тАЬIтАЩm going to lead the way.тАЭWith all four of the NBAтАЩs Conference Finals teams locked in, ticket prices on the secondary market for the NBA Finals have soared to a league record, with average ticket prices ranging from more than $3,800 to nearly $5,200, depending on where the games are played, though analysts believe that price could come down.TRAVEL & LIFESTYLEThe unofficial kickoff to the summer travel season is less than two weeks awayтАФand by all accounts, it will be a doozy. The American Automobile Association is projecting a 7% increase in travel volume for Memorial Day weekend compared to last year. All in all, 42.3 million Americans are expected to travel 50-plus miles from home for the long weekendтАФ2.7 million more than last year.Daily Cover Story ILLUSTRATION BY FORBES; PHOTO BY GETTY IMAGESA Record $3.4 Billion Haul For The WorldтАЩs 50 Highest-Paid AthletesREAD ARTICLETOPLINEEight sports. Fifteen nationalities. And 50 stars making a minimum of $45.2 million each.The world's 50 highest-paid athletes had their best year ever, raking in nearly $3.5 billion over the last 12 months (before taxes and agents' fees). That adds up to a remarkable 16% jump from 2022, itself a record year.The sums are jaw-dropping up and down the list. Let's do the numbersтАФthe top 50 collected $2.36 billion on the field from their salaries, bonuses and prize money. Off the field endorsements and other business endeavors netted a cumulative $1.08 billion.See who's No. 1, meet the five Gen Zers on the list and why only one woman made the ranks this year.WHY IT MATTERSSaudi money is supercharging athletes' payтАФand shaking up the global sports world. Star soccer players and golfers are enjoying Middle Eastern riches today, but some worry that the new, well-heeled ownership groups could destroy tomorrow's competitive balance.MOREWorld's Highest-Paid Athletes 2023: The Full List And All Our StoriesADVERTISEMENTFACTS AND COMMENTSThe urge to splurge is universal, indulged in by nearly equal measures regardless of age, income, gender or nationality, according to a new survey by Deloitte Insights of over 114,000 adults spanning 23 countries.80%Made at least one pamper purchase to lift their mood in the past month No. 1Indulgence is food & beverages, followed by clothing & accessories and personal care MenSplurge more than womenтАФand spend more when they doSTRATEGY AND SUCCESSBillionaire Harvard dropout Bill Gates shared the advice he wished тАЬI was told at the graduation I never hadтАЭ at Northern Arizona University. тАЬNot only is it okay to change your mind, reinvent yourself, or have a second career, it can be a good thing,тАЭ said Gates. For those looking for inspiration, here are five gems. VIDEOTrump Blasts Fox NewsтАФClaims Network Is тАШDesperately PushingтАЩ DeSantisWatch nowGAMESQUIZGen Z is ghosting the apparel industry. Fashion spending among 18-to-24s, traditionally its biggest fans, has declined faster than any other generation. What sector now has their buying attention, according to consumer tracking data?A.CannabisB.Live EventsC.Streaming ServicesD.ToysCheck Your AnswerFRASE BY FORBESUnscramble todayтАЩs anagram to solve this hidden phrase!Scroll to the bottom of todayтАЩs newsletter for a hint.Play Now TODAY'S PHRASE ACROSS THE NEWSROOMAnnouncing the 2023 Forbes 30 Under 30 Local Lists. WeтАЩre looking for the top young founders and leaders in the innovation hubs of Atlanta, Austin, Boston, Chicago, Cleveland, Los Angeles, Miami, Puerto Rico, Seattle and Toronto. Nominations are now open.The Forbes third annual 50 Over 50 is officially looking for its next class of women proving that success has no age limit. WeтАЩre looking for women stepping into their own after age 50. Submit your nomination today.BEYOND THE NEWSROOMThe 2023 Forbes Future Of Work Summit will take place in New York and virtually on June 1. WeтАЩll explore the forthcoming impact of artificial intelligence, shifting dynamics between workers and employers, and more. Sign up to stay tuned for updates.Thanks for reading! Follow along with us on Twitter for by-the-minute updates on the latest business and financial news throughout the day. If youтАЩre playing Frase by Forbes, todayтАЩs hint is тАЬTo be in control and make important decisions in a situation.тАЭ Good luck!Our Most Powerful Membership EverUnlock unlimited access to premium journalism, exclusive members-only events, fewer ads, premium on-demand video content and more.Become A Forbes Member For Less Than $1 A WeekUnsubscribe from Forbes Daily.Manage Email PreferencesMy Forbes Account | Newsletters | Email Help | PrivacyForbes Media 499 Washington Blvd. Jersey City, NJ 07310

Subject: Re: [opensuse] openSuSe 10.3 hangs after kernel update
Body: Am Freitag, 8. Februar 2008 schrieb Carlos E. R.: > The Thursday 2008-02-07 at 09:32 -0800, Vahe Avedissian wrote: > > I booted from the 64b installation DVD and chose boot installed system, > > and that only tried to boot the "bad" kernel (i.e. system hangs with > > same errors). > > No, you need to start the live system in the rescue dvd, mount your root > filesystem, and chown to it. Too bad, I hoped that there is still the possibility to boot the installed system with the original kernel. > As somebody else wrote (Anders Johansson): > [mount --bind] I know, that was the reason to let Vahe first try it the easy way. BTW, there is a bug report about the "missing" documentation of the rescue process when using a dynamic /dev: https://bugzilla.novell.com/show_bug.cgi?id=293691 Gru├Г┼╕ Jan -- Don't force it, get a larger hammer.

Subject: Re: [Bulk] [opensuse] A Quick Start - VirtualBox Howto
Body: Sudhir wrote: >> > > > Thank you for this post. My previous experience with virtualization > software has been frustrating, to say the list. Following your > instructions, the software installed without any problemd. > > As a new user, I tired to configure too many options at once. The only > initial configuration required was mounting the dvd drive and selecting > ALSA as the sound device. > > To access my home directory and the printer, I had to do a network > setup. Is this the the best way or are there better alternatives? > > As regards the network cards, sound cards and other such equipment, is > it possible to have direct access in vbox or does this cause problems > with the host system? > > vb allows you to specify "Shared Folders". Just open vb, select the guest os you want to specify a shared folder for and then click on the "Shared Folders" and then chose the folder you want to share between the host and guest os. I have a little network that has plenty of samba shares available so I just use a folder on a samba share. Works great both from linux and the guest os XP. I was amazed that the guest XP networking was 100% functional from within vb. No configuration required on that end. Just use windows explorer and with folder view and pick "my networks" "entire network" "windows networks" the just pick your "workgroup" expand it and all your computers are there! -- David C. Rankin, J.D., P.E. Rankin Law Firm, PLLC 510 Ochiltree Street Nacogdoches, Texas 75961 Telephone: (936) 715-9333 Facsimile: (936) 715-9339 www.rankinlawfirm.com
